docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
This is a private research repository for internal business use. For questions or corrections, contact the repository owner.

This documentation is maintained for Indonesian commercial vehicle spare parts sellers operating on Tokopedia, Shopee, and TikTok Shop.

docs.title: What is Tokopedia B2B?
docs.text:
Current Status: Tokopedia's dedicated B2B platform (tokopedia.com/b2b) appears to be inactive or redirected. The URL shows a placeholder seller page.

Alternative B2B Features on Tokopedia:

Feature Status How to Access Tokopedia B2B (tokopedia.com/b2b) Inactive/Redirected Not currently functional Tokopedia Enterprise Active but limited Contact-based, for corporate buyers Tokopedia untuk Bisnis Integrated into Seller Center Available to all sellers Grosir/Bulk Listings Active Use "Grosir" keyword in listings COD for B2B Available Enable for bulk orders

Key Finding: Tokopedia does NOT have a dedicated B2B marketplace. B2B sales happen through: 1. Regular listings marked as "Grosir" (wholesale) 2. Direct negotiation via chat 3. Custom quotation requests 4. Offline contracts (after initial contact via Tokopedia)

docs.title: How to Sell B2B on Tokopedia (Workaround)
docs.text:
Step-by-Step Guide:

  1. Create Wholesale Listings
  2. Add "Grosir" in product title (e.g., "Grosir Filter Oli Hino 10 pcs")
  3. Set wholesale pricing tiers in description

  4. Include minimum order quantity (MOQ)

  5. Enable Bulk Pricing 

    Price Example:
- 1-4 pcs: Rp 150,000/unit (retail)
- 5-9 pcs: Rp 135,000/unit (10% discount)
- 10+ pcs: Rp 120,000/unit (20% discount)

  6. Target Business Buyers

  7. Use keywords: "grosir", "partai", "wholesale", "reseller"
  8. Create separate listing for bulk orders

  9. Offer free shipping for large orders

  10. Negotiate Offline

  11. Use Tokopedia chat for initial contact
  12. Exchange business contacts for contract
  13. Process large orders via manual invoice

docs.title: 2.2 Tokopedia Enterprise / Corporate Program
docs.text:
Program Name: Tokopedia Enterprise (formerly "Tokopedia untuk Bisnis")

Target Customers: - Large corporations - Government agencies - State-owned enterprises (BUMN) - Corporate procurement

How It Works: 1. Corporate Account Registration - Submit company documents (NPWP, SIUP, NIB) - Get dedicated account manager - Access corporate pricing

  1. Custom Pricing
  2. Negotiated contract prices
  3. Volume commitments

  4. Credit terms (PO 30-60 days)

  5. Dedicated Support

  6. Priority customer service
  7. Custom invoice generation
  8. Bulk order processing

Contact Method: - Email: enterprise@tokopedia.com - Through Tokopedia official channels

Realistic Assessment: This program is primarily for LARGE buyers (hundreds of millions IDR annually). Small sellers cannot directly access enterprise features.

docs.title: 2.3 B2B Selling via Tokopedia vs Shopee
docs.text:
Feature Tokopedia Shopee Platform Focus General marketplace Social commerce Fleet Usage 45% 25% B2B Features Corporate account, Enterprise Shopee Grosir program Bulk Discount Manual negotiation Automatic tier pricing Credit Terms Possible (negotiated) Shopee PayLater Loyalty Badge system Shopee Coins Live Selling Limited Shopee Live popular Best For Established fleet buyers Price-sensitive buyers

Practical Strategy for Small-Medium Sellers (Multi-Platform):

Platform Strategy Priority Tokopedia List standard products, focus on reviews Primary platform Shopee List same products, use Shopee Coins incentive Secondary platform Shopee Live Do weekly live sales for popular items Engagement

docs.title: 2.4 B2B Selling via Tokopedia - Real Approach
docs.text:
Practical Strategy for Small-Medium Sellers:

Step Action Details 1 Create wholesale listings Use "Grosir" keyword, show bulk pricing 2 Enable B2B chat Respond to business inquiries promptly 3 Offer custom quotes For orders above Rp 5 million 4 Provide invoice For tax purposes (faktur pajak) 5 Negotiate payment terms For repeat customers (7-30 days) 6 Build relationship Move long-term contracts offline

Sample B2B Listing Format: 

Title: GROSIR Filter Oli Hino J08E 10 pcs - Ready Stock Jakarta

Description:
✓ Harga Grosir:
- 5-9 pcs: Rp 125,000/pcs
- 10-19 pcs: Rp 120,000/pcs  
- 20+ pcs: Rp 115,000/pcs (WhatsApp for price)

✓ Untuk pemesanan partai besar/regular, silahkan chat langsung
✓ Bisa request faktur pajak
✓ Pengiriman via ekspedisi trucking
✓ Melayani kebutuhan workshop & fleet operator

docs.title: 2.5 Alternative B2B Platforms
docs.text:
Better Platforms for B2B Sales:

Platform Focus B2B Features Best For Ralali.com B2B marketplace Wholesale focus, RFQ system Best for industrial B2B Mitra Bukalapak B2B wholesale Verified suppliers, bulk pricing Good for resellers PasarMikro (Bukalapak) Small business Micro-entrepreneur focus Small workshops Bizzy.co.id Business supplies Corporate procurement Medium enterprises KitaBisa B2B Business marketplace RFQ, bulk ordering General B2B Alibaba Indonesia Import/B2B Direct from factory Large volume import

Recommendation: For serious B2B selling, consider listing on Ralali.com (dedicated B2B platform) alongside Tokopedia.

docs.title: 2.5 Minimum Order Requirements (MOQ)
docs.text:
Typical MOQ by Product Category:

Product Category MOQ (Standard) Price Discount Payment Terms Filters 10 pcs 15-20% off retail COD or 7 days Brake Parts 5 sets 10-15% off retail COD or 7 days Electrical Parts 3-5 pcs 10-15% off retail COD or 7 days Engine Parts 1-3 pcs 5-10% off retail 50% down payment Turbo Chargers 1 pc Negotiated 50% down payment Full Engine Rebuild Kit 1 set Negotiated 50% down payment

For Fleet Operator Contracts:

Contract Type Monthly Volume MOQ Payment Terms Discount Small Fleet (10-30 units) Rp 10-30 million Rp 5 million/order Net 14 days 10-15% Medium Fleet (30-100 units) Rp 30-100 million Rp 10 million/order Net 30 days 15-20% Large Fleet (100+ units) Rp 100+ million Contract-based Net 30-60 days 20-30%

docs.title: 2.6 Payment Terms for Fleet Operators
docs.text:
Standard Payment Terms:

Term Type Description When Used Risk Level COD (Cash on Delivery) Payment on receipt New customers, small orders LOW Net 7 Payment within 7 days Established customers, small fleets LOW Net 14 Payment within 14 days Medium fleets, regular customers MEDIUM Net 30 Payment within 30 days Large fleets, government contracts MEDIUM Net 45-60 Payment within 45-60 days Large government contracts, BUMN HIGH 50% DP + 50% on Delivery Down payment required Large orders, new fleet customers LOW Monthly Billing Consolidated invoice Long-term contracts MEDIUM

Payment Methods Preferred by Fleet Operators:

  1. Bank Transfer (BCA, Mandiri, BNI, BRI) - Most common
  2. Company Check (Giro) - For large contracts
  3. Invoice factoring - For government contracts
  4. E-wallet (OVO, GoPay) - For small urgent orders

docs.title: 3.1 How Fleet Operators Decide Which Parts to Buy
docs.text:
Decision-Making Hierarchy:

Role Influence Decision Area Fleet Manager HIGH Overall budget, supplier selection, contract approval Procurement Officer HIGH Vendor negotiation, price comparison, order placement Workshop Supervisor MEDIUM Technical specifications, brand preference, quality check Mechanic LOW-MEDIUM Daily parts requests, urgent needs, brand familiarity

Decision Factors (Ranked):

Factor Weight Notes Price 30% Always important, but not top factor Quality/Reliability 25% Critical for safety parts (brakes, suspension) Availability/Lead Time 20% Vehicle downtime costs money Brand Trust 15% OEM vs aftermarket preference Payment Terms 10% Important for cash flow management

docs.title: 3.2 OEM vs Aftermarket Preference
docs.text:
Preference by Fleet Type:

Fleet Type OEM Preference Aftermarket Preference Notes Government Bus (TransJakarta, DAMRI) 80-90% 10-20% Required by regulation/audit Large Corporate Fleet 60-70% 30-40% Quality-focused, budget available E-commerce Logistics 40-50% 50-60% Cost-conscious, high volume Mining Fleet 70-80% 20-30% Harsh conditions, reliability critical Plantation Fleet 30-40% 60-70% Cost-conscious, remote locations Inter-city Bus 40-50% 50-60% Mixed, depends on owner Small Trucking (Owner-operator) 20-30% 70-80% Cost is primary factor

Key Insight: - Government fleets = ALMOST ALL OEM - Private fleets = MIXED (trend toward aftermarket for cost) - E-commerce boom = HIGH OPPORTUNITY for aftermarket parts

docs.title: 3.3 Order Cycle Frequency
docs.text:
Typical Order Cycles:

Fleet Size Filter Orders Brake Parts Major Repairs Full Service Small (10-30 units) Monthly Monthly Quarterly Bi-annually Medium (30-100 units) Bi-weekly Monthly Monthly Quarterly Large (100+ units) Weekly Bi-weekly Monthly Monthly

Seasonal Variations:

Season Demand Change Reason January-February LOW (-20%) Post-holiday slowdown March-April MEDIUM Pre-Eid preparations May-June HIGH (+30%) Post-Eid repairs, peak logistics July-August MEDIUM Normal operations September-October HIGH (+25%) Year-end fleet maintenance November-December VERY HIGH (+40%) Budget clearing, holiday logistics

docs.title: 3.4 Purchasing Decision Makers
docs.text:
Who Makes the Purchase?

Fleet Type Primary Decision Maker Secondary Approval Required Government Bus Procurement Division Workshop Supervisor Director/Finance Large Corporate Procurement Manager Fleet Manager CFO (above Rp 50M) E-commerce Logistics Procurement Lead Maintenance Supervisor Finance Manager Mining Fleet Maintenance Superintendent Procurement Officer Mine Manager Plantation Fleet Estate Manager Workshop Foreman Regional Manager Inter-city Bus Owner/Director Workshop Supervisor Owner Small Trucking Owner-Operator Mechanic Self

Buying Process (Large Fleets):

1. Maintenance Request → Workshop Supervisor
2. Technical Specification → Workshop Foreman
3. RFQ (Request for Quotation) → Procurement Officer
4. Price Comparison → Procurement Manager
5. Supplier Selection → Fleet Manager Approval
6. Purchase Order → Finance Verification
7. Payment Processing → Finance Director (if above threshold)
8. Delivery & Inspection → Workshop
9. Invoice Processing → Finance
10. Payment → Net 30-60 days

docs.title: Government Procurement System (LKPP)
docs.text:
The National Public Procurement Agency (LKPP) oversees all government procurement.

Key Portal: SIRUP (Sistem Informasi Rencana Umum Pengadaan) - https://sirup.lkpp.go.id (main portal) - Shows all planned government procurements

Procurement Methods:

Method Threshold Process Direct Appointment Below Rp 200 million Direct negotiation Quick Tender Rp 200 million - Rp 2 billion Faster process, limited bidders Open Tender Above Rp 2 billion Full public bidding E-Tendering All above threshold Electronic bidding via LPSE

docs.title: How to Participate in Government Tenders
docs.text:
Step 1: Register as Vendor

  1. Visit the e-procurement portal:
  2. TransJakarta: https://eproc.transjakarta.co.id
  3. DAMRI: https://eproc.damri.co.id
  4. LPSE Jakarta: https://lpse.jakarta.go.id

  5. LPSE National: https://inaproc.lkpp.go.id

  6. Register vendor account (requires):

  7. NPWP (Tax ID)
  8. NIB (Business Identification Number)
  9. SIUP/NIB (Business License)
  10. Company deed (Akta Perusahaan)
  11. Bank account in company name

  12. Tax clearance (SPPKP)

  13. Complete vendor qualification:

  14. Small business (Usaha Kecil)
  15. Non-small business (Non-UK)
  16. Able to bid on appropriate categories

Step 2: Find Relevant Tenders

  1. Monitor tender announcements:
  2. Check SIRUP for planned procurements
  3. Subscribe to tender notifications

  4. Join industry associations for leads

  5. Download tender documents:

  6. Free download from portal
  7. Review specifications
  8. Check eligibility requirements

Step 3: Prepare Bid

Required documents typically include: - Technical proposal (product specifications) - Price proposal (HPS - Harga Perkiraan Sendiri) - Company profile & financial statements - Track record/references - Product samples (for evaluation) - Certifications (if required)

Step 4: Submit Bid

  • Upload to e-procurement system
  • Submit before deadline
  • Pay bid security (jasa penawaran) - typically 1-3% of bid value

Step 5: Auction & Evaluation

  • Price auction (if multiple qualified bidders)
  • Technical evaluation
  • Price comparison
  • Winner announcement

docs.title: 4.2 Government Fleet Tender Examples
docs.text:
Recent DAMRI Tender (from eproc.damri.co.id):

Tender Title Category Est. Budget Status PENGADAAN SUKU CADANG KENDERAAN MERK MERCEDES BENZ MELALUI PROGRAM ON SITE PART STOCK (OSPS) Spare Parts Multi-year contract Active PENGADAAN REKONDISI BUS RAPID TRANSIT (BRT) HINO R.260 Bus Rebuild Rp 2-5 billion Completed PENGADAAN PERALATAN BENGKEL UNTUK KANTOR CABANG Workshop Equipment Rp 500 million Completed

OSPS (On-Site Part Stock) Program: - Vendor supplies parts to be stored at fleet operator's warehouse - Payment made when parts are used (consignment model) - Long-term contract (1-3 years) - Requires vendor to maintain stock levels - High commitment but guaranteed demand

docs.title: 4.3 Certifications Required for Government Contracts
docs.text:
Minimum Requirements:

Requirement Description Where to Obtain NIB (Nomor Induk Berusaha) Business registration OSS (Online Single Submission) NPWP Perusahaan Company tax ID Tax office (KPP) SIUP/NIB Business license OSS system TDP Company registration Not needed if have NIB SKT (Sertifikat Keterangan Terdaftar) Vendor registration LPSE portal SPPKP Taxable entrepreneur status Tax office

Additional for Larger Contracts:

Requirement Description Notes ISO 9001 Quality management For contracts above Rp 1 billion ISO 14001 Environmental management For certain categories TKDN (Tingkat Komponen Dalam Negeri) Local content requirement Minimum 40% for certain products Product Certification (SNI) Indonesian National Standard For safety-critical parts Authorized Distributor Letter From brand principal For OEM parts

docs.title: 4.4 Tender Strategy for Spare Parts Sellers
docs.text:
Tier 1: Start Small (Rp 50-200 million contracts)

  • Target: Regional government fleets, city transport
  • Focus: Filters, brake parts, consumables
  • Competition: Lower
  • Win probability: Higher

Tier 2: Medium Contracts (Rp 200 million - Rp 1 billion)

  • Target: Provincial fleets, DAMRI regional
  • Focus: Complete maintenance packages
  • Requirements: Track record, references
  • Competition: Medium

Tier 3: Large Contracts (Rp 1+ billion)

  • Target: TransJakarta, DAMRI national, BUMN
  • Focus: Long-term supply contracts (OSPS)
  • Requirements: ISO certification, large capital
  • Competition: High (limited to established players)

Practical Approach: 1. Start with direct sales to fleet workshops 2. Build track record (2-3 years) 3. Register on LPSE portals 4. Bid on small tenders first 5. Build relationships with procurement officers 6. Scale up to larger contracts

docs.title: 5.1 Authorized Service Center Network
docs.text:
Major Brand Authorized Workshops:

Brand # of Authorized Workshops Coverage Parts Supply Method Hino (Hino Service Center) 200+ National Direct from Hino Indonesia Isuzu (Isuzu Service Center) 150+ National Direct from Isuzu Astra Mitsubishi FUSO (FUSO Authorized) 180+ National Direct from Krama Yudha Mercedes-Benz 80+ Java, Sumatra Direct from Mercedes-Benz Indonesia Scania 40+ Major cities Direct from Scania Indonesia Toyota (Daihatsu trucks) 500+ National Direct from Toyota Astra

Key Insight: Authorized service centers MUST use OEM parts. They cannot legally buy aftermarket parts. Their parts supply comes exclusively from the brand principal.

docs.title: 5.2 Independent Service Centers (Bengkel Umum)
docs.text:
Independent Workshop Types:

Type Number (Est.) Parts Source Target Market Large Independent Workshop 2,000+ Wholesale distributors, aftermarket Medium fleets Medium Workshop 5,000+ Wholesalers, Tokopedia, market Small fleets, owner-operators Small Workshop (Bengkel Pinggir Jalan) 20,000+ Market, retail shops Owner-operators, individual trucks Mobile Mechanic 10,000+ Parts stores, emergency purchases Roadside repairs

Independent Workshop Parts Needs: - Mostly aftermarket parts (cost-conscious customers) - Fast delivery (workshop cannot wait) - Wide range of brands (various truck models) - Technical advice/guidance

docs.title: 5.3 Can You Supply to Authorized Service Centers?
docs.text:
Official Answer: NO

Authorized service centers have exclusive supply agreements with brand principals. They cannot legally buy from independent suppliers.

Exceptions (Limited): - Emergency parts when principal is out of stock - Non-warranty repairs on older vehicles - Used/older vehicles no longer supported by principal

Realistic Opportunity:

Scenario Opportunity Level Notes OEM parts supply ZERO Must be authorized distributor Aftermarket to authorized centers ALMOST ZERO Contractually forbidden Supply to independent workshops HIGH Primary market for aftermarket Supply to fleet in-house workshops VERY HIGH Major opportunity

docs.title: 5.4 Documentation Required for B2B Transactions
docs.text:
Standard Business Documents:

Document Purpose When Required Faktur Pajak (Tax Invoice) Tax deduction All B2B transactions Surat Jalan (Delivery Note) Proof of delivery All shipments Invoice Payment request All transactions Kuitansi (Receipt) Payment acknowledgment Cash transactions Purchase Order (PO) Customer order confirmation Large orders, contracts Quotation/Penawaran Price proposal B2B negotiations BAST (Berita Acara Serah Terima) Delivery acceptance Large contracts Garansi/Warranty Certificate Product warranty Engine parts, electronics

For Government Contracts: - All above plus: - Surat Penawaran (Official bid letter) - Jaminan Penawaran (Bid security) - Jaminan Pelaksanaan (Performance bond) - Surat Perjanjian (Contract agreement)

docs.title: 6.2 Fleet Hub Locations

docs.title: SECTION 7: ACTION PLAN FOR B2B SALES
7.1 Getting Started - 3-Month Plan
docs.text:
Month 1: Foundation

Week Action Details 1 Set up business documents NIB, NPWP, faktur pajak capability 2 Create B2B-focused listings Wholesale prices, MOQ clearly stated 3 Identify target fleets List 20 potential fleet customers 4 Direct outreach Visit workshops, introduce products

Month 2: Build Relationships

Week Action Details 5 Offer trial orders Small orders to build trust 6 Collect testimonials Document satisfied customers 7 Follow up consistently Weekly check-ins with prospects 8 Negotiate first contracts Monthly supply agreements

Month 3: Scale Up

Week Action Details 9 Register on LPSE portals Prepare for government tenders 10 Expand product range Add high-demand items 11 Formalize contracts Written agreements, payment terms 12 Seek referrals Ask satisfied customers for leads

docs.title: 7.2 Target Customer Segments (Priority Order)
docs.text: Priority Segment Why Approach 1 E-commerce logistics fleets Fastest growing, cost-conscious Competitive pricing, fast delivery 2 Independent workshops Regular buyers, wide reach Volume discounts, technical support 3 Medium-sized logistics companies Balance of volume and price flexibility Relationship building 4 Plantation fleets (Sumatra) Large volume, remote locations Delivery capability, credit terms 5 Mining fleets (Kalimantan) High-value, harsh conditions Heavy-duty parts, reliability focus 6 Bus companies Regular maintenance cycle OEM alternatives, nationwide shipping 7 Government fleets (long-term) Large contracts, stable demand Tender participation, certifications

docs.title: 7.3 Key Success Factors for B2B Sales
docs.text:
1. Price Competitiveness - Offer 15-25% below retail for bulk orders - Match wholesale market prices - Consider volume-based pricing tiers

2. Delivery Speed - Same-day delivery for Jakarta area - 1-2 days for Java, Sumatra - Use multiple logistics partners (truck, train, cargo)

3. Product Quality - Stand behind products with warranty - Replace defective parts quickly - Build reputation for reliability

4. Payment Flexibility - Offer credit terms for established customers - Accept multiple payment methods - Consider installment for large orders

5. Technical Support - Provide product specifications - Offer installation guidance - Help with parts identification

6. Relationship Building - Regular customer visits - WhatsApp group for updates - Holiday greetings and appreciation

docs.title: Top 10 Fleet Demand Insights
docs.text:
1. Government fleets (TransJakarta, DAMRI) buy via TENDER only - Must register on e-procurement portals

  2. E-commerce logistics has BOOMED 150-500% since 2020 - Best growth opportunity

  3. Mining fleets need HEAVY-DUTY parts - Higher price tolerance, reliability focus

  4. Palm oil fleets in Sumatra/Kalimantan = HIGH VOLUME - Cost-conscious, remote locations

  5. Tokopedia does NOT have dedicated B2B platform - Use "Grosir" listings and direct chat

  6. Government contracts require NIB, NPWP, ISO certifications - Prepare documents early

  7. Authorized service centers = LOCKED to OEM - Focus on independent workshops instead

  8. Jakarta/Sunter = National parts hub - Best wholesale sourcing location

  9. EURO 4 parts demand growing in urban Java - Premium market segment

  10. Payment terms critical for fleet operators - Net 14-30 days standard for contracts

Report Generated: April 7, 2026 Data Sources: Government e-procurement portals, industry research, marketplace analysis Note: Fleet sizes and demand are estimates based on available data and industry knowledge.

PROS ✓
docs.text:
1. Largest Buyer Base: 100M+ monthly active users
  2. B2B Features: Corporate purchasing, bulk orders supported
  3. Trust System: Power Merchant badges increase buyer confidence
  4. Fleet Manager Reach: Many fleet operators use Tokopedia for procurement
  5. Brand Recognition: Most trusted e-commerce in Indonesia
  6. Payment Options: Corporate invoicing available for registered businesses
  7. Analytics: Detailed sales and performance metrics
  8. Cross-selling: Related product recommendations

docs.title: CONS ✗
docs.text:
1. High Competition: 300-500 sellers per category
  2. Commission Fees: 4-6% reduces margins on low-cost parts
  3. Price War: Automatic price comparison drives prices down
  4. Storage Costs: Power Merchant requires fast shipping (need warehouse)
  5. Returns: High return rates for misidentified parts
  6. TopAds Required: Organic reach declining, paid ads necessary
  7. Customer Service: 24/7 chat response expected

docs.title: 6.2 Should You Sell on All Platforms or Focus on One?
docs.text: RECOMMENDATION: MULTI-PLATFORM WITH PRIORITY

docs.title: Strategy A: Focused Tokopedia (Recommended for Start)
docs.text:
Investment: Rp 50-100 million inventory

Platform Priority Reason Tokopedia 90% Primary focus, best ROI Shopee 10% Test market, backup channel

Benefits: - Focused operations - Lower complexity - Better inventory management - Dedicated customer service

docs.title: Strategy B: Multi-Platform (Recommended for Scale)
docs.text:
Investment: Rp 200-500 million inventory

Platform Priority Product Focus Monthly Target Tokopedia 60% All products Rp 100M GMV Shopee 30% Fast-moving parts Rp 50M GMV OLX 5% Used parts Rp 10M GMV Facebook 5% Community sales Rp 5M GMV

Benefits: - Maximum reach - Risk diversification - Different buyer segments - Higher total revenue

docs.title: 6.3 How to Manage Inventory Across Multiple Platforms
Option 1: Manual Management (Low Volume)
docs.text:
• Use spreadsheet to track stock
  • Manual update each platform
  • Suitable for < 100 SKUs
  • Cost: Rp 0 (free)

docs.title: Option 2: Inventory Management Software (Medium Volume)
docs.text:
• Use software like SIRCLO, Ginee, or Sellcentre
  • Sync inventory across platforms
  • Automated stock updates
  • Suitable for 100-1,000 SKUs
  • Cost: Rp 500,000 - 2,000,000/month

docs.title: Option 3: ERP Integration (High Volume)
docs.text:
• Use Odoo, Zoho, or custom ERP
  • Full inventory, accounting, CRM
  • Suitable for 1,000+ SKUs
  • Cost: Rp 5,000,000 - 20,000,000/month

Recommended Stack:

Volume Inventory Tool Cost Platform Integration Startup (< Rp 50M/month) Google Sheets + Manual Free Manual Growing (Rp 50-200M/month) SIRCLO or Ginee Rp 500K-1M/month Tokopedia, Shopee Scale (Rp 200M-1B/month) Sellcentre Pro Rp 2M-5M/month All platforms Enterprise (> Rp 1B/month) Custom ERP Rp 10M+/month Full integration

docs.text:

FINDING: Shopee has LESS COMPETITION for high-value technical parts, but Tokopedia has MORE BUYERS.

Strategy: - Fast-moving consumables (filters, belts): Tokopedia only (volume) - High-value technical parts: Tokopedia + Shopee (capture both markets) - Specialty parts: Shopee first, Tokopedia second

docs.title: Commercial Vehicle Spare Parts | Indonesia | April 2026
docs.text: Research Date: April 7, 2026 Focus: Commercial Vehicle Parts (Trucks, Buses) Data Source: Tokopedia marketplace analysis, industry estimates

docs.title: SECTION 1: TYPICAL WHOLESALE vs RETAIL MARGINS
1.1 Supply Chain Price Tiers
docs.text:
The Indonesian automotive parts supply chain typically has 4-5 price tiers:

IMPORTER/PRINCIPAL (Cost + 10-15%)
        ↓
MASTER DISTRIBUTOR (Cost + 20-30%)
        ↓
REGIONAL WHOLESALER (Cost + 35-50%)
        ↓
RETAILER/SHOP (Cost + 60-100%)
        ↓
END CONSUMER

docs.title: ENGINE PARTS (High Technical Value)
docs.text:
Product Distributor Cost Wholesale Price Retail Price Retail Margin Volume Turbo Charger Hino J08E Rp 1,800,000 Rp 2,400,000 Rp 3,000,000 67% Medium Turbo Cartridge Rp 1,000,000 Rp 1,300,000 Rp 1,700,000 70% Medium Injector Common Rail Isuzu Rp 2,200,000 Rp 3,000,000 Rp 3,600,000 64% Low Piston Kit Hino J08E Rp 1,500,000 Rp 2,200,000 Rp 3,000,000 100% Low Liner Set 4D34T Rp 2,200,000 Rp 2,800,000 Rp 3,400,000 55% Low

KEY FINDING: Engine parts have 55-100% retail margins due to: - Technical expertise required - Low price sensitivity (truck owners need parts urgently) - Limited seller competition - High warranty risk (sellers need margin buffer)

docs.title: BRAKE PARTS (High Volume, High Competition)
docs.text:
Product Distributor Cost Wholesale Price Retail Price Retail Margin Volume Kampas Kopling Hino Lohan 15" Rp 450,000 Rp 650,000 Rp 850,000 89% High Brake Lining Canter PS125 Rp 300,000 Rp 450,000 Rp 650,000 117% High Brake Drum Canter Rp 400,000 Rp 600,000 Rp 800,000 100% Medium Brake Pad Hino 500 Rp 250,000 Rp 350,000 Rp 450,000 80% High Clutch Cover PS125 Rp 500,000 Rp 700,000 Rp 1,000,000 100% Medium

KEY FINDING: Brake parts have 80-117% retail margins: - High replacement frequency (every 30,000-50,000 km) - Multiple brands compete (OEM, aftermarket) - Price-conscious buyers but need reliability - High return rates require margin buffer

docs.title: FILTERS (Highest Volume, Lowest Margin)
docs.text:
Product Distributor Cost Wholesale Price Retail Price Retail Margin Volume Oil Filter Hino J08E Rp 85,000 Rp 110,000 Rp 150,000 76% Very High Oil Filter Isuzu 4HK1 Rp 80,000 Rp 100,000 Rp 140,000 75% Very High Fuel Filter Hino 500 Rp 120,000 Rp 150,000 Rp 200,000 67% High Air Filter Hino Lohan Rp 150,000 Rp 200,000 Rp 280,000 87% High Fuel Filter Isuzu NKR Rp 100,000 Rp 125,000 Rp 180,000 80% High

KEY FINDING: Filters have 67-87% retail margins: - Highest volume (replacement every 10,000-20,000 km) - Lowest absolute profit (Rp 50,000-100,000 per unit) - Brand matters less (many interchangeable) - Competition is intense

Wholesale Pricing Evidence from Tokopedia: - Oil Filter Hino Dutro (Grosir 10 pcs): Rp 1,209,000 = Rp 120,900/unit - Oil Filter Hino Dutro (Grosir 5 pcs): Rp 628,000 = Rp 125,600/unit - Single unit retail price: Rp 150,000-180,000

Volume Discount Calculation: - Buying 5 pcs: Rp 125,600/unit (16% cheaper than retail) - Buying 10 pcs: Rp 120,900/unit (20% cheaper than retail)

docs.title: ELECTRICAL PARTS (High Margin, Technical)
docs.text:
Product Distributor Cost Wholesale Price Retail Price Retail Margin Volume Alternator Hino J08E 24V Rp 1,200,000 Rp 1,600,000 Rp 2,350,000 96% Low Alternator Genuine Hino Rp 4,500,000 Rp 5,200,000 Rp 6,000,000 33% Very Low Starter Isuzu 4HK1 Rp 1,000,000 Rp 1,400,000 Rp 1,700,000 70% Medium Solenoid Switch 4HK1 Rp 200,000 Rp 280,000 Rp 377,000 89% Medium Armature Starter 4HK1 Rp 350,000 Rp 450,000 Rp 510,000 46% Low

KEY FINDING: Electrical parts have 33-96% retail margins: - Genuine parts have lower margins (33-50%) - Aftermarket/replacement parts have higher margins (70-96%) - Technical knowledge required reduces competition - High warranty claims on cheap alternatives

Tokopedia Price Range Evidence (Alternator Hino J08E): - Lowest price: Rp 1,500,000 (aftermarket/repair unit) - Average price: Rp 1,800,000-2,350,000 (aftermarket OEM spec) - Highest price: Rp 6,000,000 (Genuine Hino) - Price spread: 300% difference between cheapest and genuine!

docs.text:

Evidence from Tokopedia Wholesale Listings: - Oil Filter Hino (10 pcs): Rp 1,209,000 = Rp 120,900/unit (19% below retail) - Fan Belt Isuzu NMR71 (10 pcs): Rp 888,000 = Rp 88,800/unit (vs retail Rp 100,000-120,000) - Fan Belt Panther (5 pcs): Rp 349,000 = Rp 69,800/unit (vs retail Rp 90,000)

docs.text:
FILTERS (Shopee Grosir Program): | Product | 1 pcs | 5 pcs | 10 pcs | Provider | Coins | |---------|-------|-------|--------|----------|-------| | Oil Filter Hino J08E | Rp 145,000 | Rp 125,000 | Rp 115,000 | Jakarta Filter | 2% | | Air Filter FM260 | Rp 285,000 | Rp 245,000 | Rp 225,000 | Filter Truk ID | 2% |

Benefits of Monthly Commitments: 1. Priority stock allocation (rare items reserved) 2. Better payment terms (credit 30-60 days) 3. Technical support and training 4. Marketing materials and point-of-sale display 5. Free shipping for large orders

docs.title: 2.4 Seasonal Pricing Variations
docs.text:
Season Price Trend Reasoning Jan-Mar (Rainy Season) Stable to +5% Less maintenance, slower sales Apr-Jun (Pre-Harvest) +10-15% Increased demand from logistics Jul-Sep (Dry Season) +5-10% Higher vehicle usage, more breakdowns Oct-Dec (Year End) +15-20% Budget clearing, fleet maintenance

Best Buying Months: - January-February: Slowest sales, best negotiation leverage - Post-Lebaran (May-June): Many promotions

Highest Price Months: - September-October: Peak logistics season - December: Year-end fleet maintenance rush

docs.title: SECTION 3: ONLINE vs OFFLINE PRICE COMPARISON
3.1 Jakarta Offline Market Hubs
docs.text:
Key Locations:

Location Specialty Price Level Sunter (Jalan Industri Sunter) Complete truck parts, new & used 15-20% below online Cilincing (Jalan Raya Cilincing) Heavy equipment, bus parts 10-15% below online Pasar Mobil Kemayoran Used parts, accessories 30-50% below online Cipete/Lenteng Agung Alternator, starter specialists 10-20% below online Mangga Dua Consumer car parts, accessories 5-10% below online

docs.title: 3.2 Price Comparison: Online vs Offline
docs.text:
General Rule:

Offline prices are 10-20% CHEAPER than online for common parts, but online prices are 10-30% CHEAPER for rare/specialized parts.

Product Category Online (Tokopedia) Offline (Sunter) Price Difference Oil Filter Hino Rp 150,000 Rp 130,000 Online +15% Fuel Filter Isuzu Rp 180,000 Rp 160,000 Online +13% Air Filter Hino Rp 280,000 Rp 250,000 Online +12% Brake Lining Canter Rp 650,000 Rp 600,000 Online +8% Kampas Kopling Hino Rp 850,000 Rp 800,000 Online +6% Alternator Hino J08E Rp 2,350,000 Rp 2,500,000 Offline +6% Turbo Hino 500 Rp 3,000,000 Rp 3,200,000 Offline +7% Injector Isuzu 4HK1 Rp 3,600,000 Rp 3,800,000 Offline +6% Radiator Hino 500 Rp 2,500,000 Rp 2,300,000 Online +9% Starter Isuzu 4HK1 Rp 1,700,000 Rp 1,800,000 Offline +6%

docs.title: 3.3 Why the Price Differences?
docs.text:
Offline Cheaper (Consumables): - No platform commission (4-6% Tokopedia/Shopee) - Cash transactions (no payment gateway fees) - Bulk purchasing power - Direct negotiation (price flexibility) - No shipping costs for local buyers

Online Cheaper (Specialized Parts): - Wider seller competition (price comparison) - Direct from distributor (skip middleman) - Lower overhead (no physical shop) - Promotion/voucher discounts - Free shipping subsidies

docs.title: 6.1 Summary: Where to Make Money
docs.text:
BEST PROFIT MARGINS: 1. Brake Parts (80-117% margin) - High volume, moderate competition 2. Electrical Parts (70-96% margin) - Technical expertise required, low competition 3. Filters (67-87% margin) - Highest volume, easiest entry

BEST REVENUE POTENTIAL: 1. Engine Parts (Rp 2-3.5M per sale) - High value, loyal customers 2. Electrical Parts (Rp 1.5-2.5M per sale) - Good balance of value and volume 3. Brake Parts (Rp 450K-1M per sale) - High volume compensates lower price

BEST ENTRY POINT FOR NEW SELLERS: → Start with FILTERS (highest volume, easiest to source, moderate competition) → Add BRAKE PARTS (good margins, high volume, established demand) → Add ELECTRICAL (high margins, differentiation opportunity)

docs.title: 7.1 Euro 4 Considerations
docs.text:
• Euro 4 vehicles (2018+) require:
  • Fuel water separator
  • MAF/MAP sensors
  • Crankshaft position sensor
  • Specialized fuel filters
  • These parts are more expensive and less available
  • Consider stocking for fleet customers

docs.title: 7.2 Battery Configuration
docs.text:
• Light Trucks: Single N150 or N120 (12V)
  • Medium Trucks: 2x N200 (24V system)
  • Heavy Trucks/Bus: 2x N200 or N250 (24V system)
  • Typical replacement: 1-2 years

docs.title: 7.3 ECU/ECM Warning
docs.text:
• ECU replacement requires:
  • Dealer programming
  • VIN-specific coding
  • Security code pairing
  • Not recommended for self-service
  • Consider remanufactured units for cost savings

docs.title: 7.4 Radiator Options
docs.text:
• New Complete: Rp 2.5M - Rp 4M
  • Core Replacement: Rp 1.5M - Rp 2.5M
  • Recoring Service: Rp 1M - Rp 1.8M
  • Consider recoring for budget-conscious fleets

docs.title: Brand Authorization Requirements for Commercial Vehicle Parts
Indonesia Market - Official Dealer vs Independent Seller Guide
docs.text: Scope: Tokopedia, Shopee, TikTok Shop brand policies Focus: Hino, Isuzu, Mitsubishi Fuso, Toyota, Denso, Bosch, Garrett, NPR Last Updated: April 8, 2026

docs.title: 1.1 Hino Motors (Toyota Group)
docs.text:
Official Channel: - Authorized Dealer Network: PT Hino Motors Sales Indonesia - Distribution: Direct from factory (Karawang Plant) - Product Categories: Genuine Hino, Hino Genuine Parts

Authorization Requirements: | Requirement | Mandatory | Notes | |-------------|-----------|-------| | Official Dealer Contract | YES | From Hino branch office | | Authorized Workshop Status | RECOMMENDED | For technical parts | | Volume Commitment | YES | Minimum Rp 1B/year | | Storage Facility Audit | YES | Must meet Hino standards | | Training Certification | RECOMMENDED | Product knowledge |

Evidence Documents: - Surat Keagenan Resmi (Authorized Agent Letter) - NIB with KBLI matching - NPWP perusahaan - Foto gudang dan toko - Rekening atas nama perusahaan

Penalty for Unauthorized Selling: - Product takedown - Shop suspension (3 days to permanent) - Legal action possible for counterfeit claims

docs.title: 1.2 Isuzu Astra Motor Indonesia (IAMI)
docs.text:
Official Channel: - Principal: PT Isuzu Astra Motor Indonesia - Distribution: Distributor network + Authorized workshops - Parts Categories: Genuine Isuzu, Isuzu Genuine Parts, NBC

For Independent Sellers: Cannot sell "Genuine Isuzu" without authorization. Must use: - "Aftermarket" label - Compatible/Replacement designation - Cannot use Isuzu logo in images

docs.title: 2. SELLING OEM PARTS WITHOUT DEALERSHIP STATUS
2.1 Legal Workarounds
docs.text:
Method 1: Parallel Import - Source from authorized dealers overseas - Import with proper documentation (PIB) - Sell with "Import" designation - Legal as long as parts are genuine

Method 2: Supply Chain Resale - Buy from authorized workshops/distributors - Keep supplier invoice - Mark up and resell - Legal (first sale doctrine applies)

Method 3: Used/Refurbished Parts - "Copotan" from accident vehicles - Reconditioned parts - Clearly stated as used - More lenient policies

docs.title: 2.2 Description Strategy
docs.text:
FORBIDDEN words without authorization: - "Genuine [Brand]" - "Original [Brand]" - "Resmi" - "Dealer" - "Agen resmi"

ALLOWED alternatives: - "Compatible with [Brand]" - "Replacement for [Brand]" - "Aftermarket" - "Type [Brand]"

docs.title: 3.1 Valid Supplier Invoice Template
docs.text: Must Include: - Supplier business name and NPWP - Invoice number and date - Part number and description - Quantity - Unit price and total - Stamp/live signature

docs.title: 3.2 Import Documentation (PIB)
docs.text: Required Elements: - Shipper name and address - Consignee (your business) - HS Code (tarif pos) - Part descriptions - CIF value in USD - Import duty and tax (PPH, PPN)

docs.title: 4.1 Identifying Counterfeit Parts
docs.text:
Visual Indicators: | Aspect | Genuine | KW/Counterfeit | |--------|---------|----------------| | Box Quality | Thick cardboard, glossy print | Thin box, dull/faded | | Logo Printing | Sharp, embossed | Blurry, flat print | | Part Number | Laser etched, consistent | Printed, irregular | | Serial Number | Unique, verifiable online | Generic or missing | | Packaging Seal | Holographic, tamper-proof | Plain tape, easily opened | | Weight | Matches OEM spec | Often lighter | | Surface Finish | Smooth, precise machining | Rough, irregular |

Price Red Flags: - Below 50 percent of market price - "Too good to be true" deals - Bulk pricing suspiciously low - "Clearance sale" from unknown source

docs.title: 4.2 Proving Authenticity
docs.text:
For Marketplaces: 1. Purchase Documentation - Supplier invoice with NPWP - Import documents if applicable - Authorized dealer receipt

  1. Product Documentation
  2. Manufacturer certificates
  3. Test reports

  4. MSDS (for applicable parts)

  5. Verification Channels

  6. Official brand hotline
  7. Website serial check

  8. Authorized distributor confirmation

  9. Physical Verification

  10. Side-by-side comparison photos
  11. Magnified detail shots
  12. Weight vs official spec comparison

docs.title: 5.1 Creating Brand Independence
docs.text:
Private Label Approach: 1. Source quality aftermarket parts 2. Create own brand name (e.g., "TrukPro", "Maju Diesel") 3. Custom packaging with your brand 4. Build reputation on quality, not OEM association

Benefits: - No authorization needed - Higher margins - Less price competition - Customer loyalty to your brand

docs.title: 5.2 Authorized Partnership Path
docs.text:
Steps to Become Authorized: 1. Legal Entity Setup - PT (Perseroan Terbatas) formation - NIB registration - VAT (PKP) registration - Business bank account

  1. Facility Requirements
  2. Minimum 200m2 warehouse
  3. Office space
  4. Security system

  5. Inventory management system

  6. Financial Requirements

  7. Audited statements
  8. Bank guarantee
  9. Working capital proof (Rp 500M+)

  10. Credit references

  11. Application Process

  12. Submit to brand directly
  13. Interview with regional manager
  14. Site visit by brand representative
  15. Training program attendance
  16. Sign multi-year agreement

Timeline and Costs: | Brand | Application Fee | Inventory Requirement | Timeline | |-------|-------------------|----------------------|----------| | Hino | Rp 50M | Rp 1B | 3-6 months | | Isuzu | Rp 25M | Rp 500M | 2-4 months | | Mitsubishi | Rp 30M | Rp 750M | 3-5 months |

docs.title: 6.1 Takedown Response Template
docs.text:
Subject: Banding Penghapusan Produk - [Product Name]

Body: Yth. Tim Tokopedia,

Saya mengajukan banding terhadap penghapusan produk [NAMA PRODUK].

Alasan penghapusan yang diterima: [ALASAN]

Penjelasan saya: 1. Produk ini adalah [compatible/aftermarket/second] 2. Bukan klaim "Genuine/Original" dari brand 3. Sumber: [distributor/workshop/import] 4. Lampiran bukti: - Invoice supplier: [file] - PIB import: [file] - Foto produk asli: [file]

Mohon kiranya produk dapat diaktifkan kembali.

Hormat saya, [Nama] [Nama Toko] [Nomor HP]

docs.title: 7.1 Denso Japan
docs.text:
Indonesia Presence: - PT Denso Indonesia (Karawang) - Aftermarket via PT Astra Denso Indonesia

docs.title: 7.2 Bosch Germany
docs.text:
Indonesia Operation: - PT Robert Bosch (Tangerang) - Aftermarket: Bosch Automotive Aftermarket

Requires Authorization: - Brake components - Sensors (OEM) - Fuel injectors (some)

Open to Independent Sales: - Tools - Generic parts (brake fluid) - Some aftermarket brake pads

docs.title: 7.3 Garrett Turbo (Honeywell)
docs.text:
Distribution: PT Turbo Solutions Indonesia

Strict Authorization Required: - Garrett branded turbos - Original equipment parts - "Meleq" designation

Aftermarket Options (No Authorization): - Turbo repair kits - Compatible replacement turbos - "Turbo type Garrett"

docs.title: 7.4 Mitsubishi Fuso (PT KTB)
docs.text:
Distribution Structure: - Principal: PT Krama Yudha Tiga Berlian - Network: Dealer + Sub-dealer - Categories: Original, Genuine Parts, Value Parts

Requirements for Authorization: - Rp 250M minimum stock - Workshop facility (for commercial) - Geographic exclusivity commitment - Sales target agreement (Rp 2B/year)

docs.title: Product Listing Guide - Commercial Vehicle Spare Parts
Tokopedia and Marketplace SEO Best Practices for Truck Parts
docs.text: Last Updated: April 8, 2026 Target: Hino, Isuzu, Mitsubishi trucks, buses, heavy equipment

docs.title: 1.1 Basic Title Structure
docs.text:
Formula: [NAMA PRODUK] + [MOBIL/TRUK] + [MEREK/TYPE] + [PART NUMBER] + [KUALITAS/KONDISI] + [GARANSI]

Example Titles: - Bad: Injector Hino - Good: Injector Assembly Truk Hino 500 Ranger J08E 095000-6593 Denso Quality - GARANSI 30 HARI - Bad: Turbo Hino - Good: Turbocharger Hino 500 FM260 GT3576DL Euro 3 Original Garrett Type - Bad: Piston Ring Hino - Good: Piston Ring Set NPR Hino Lohan J08E 6 Silinder S1301-92080 - Made in Japan

docs.title: 1.2 Title Length Optimization
docs.text:
Tokopedia: - Maximum: 120 characters (shown in search) - Recommended: 70-100 characters - Include: Product name, vehicle, part number - Front-load keywords

Shopee: - Maximum: 120 characters - Hashtags allowed in title - Use keywords that buyers search

TikTok Shop: - Maximum: 80 characters (mobile-first) - Shorter, punchier titles - Emojis can help (1-2 max)

docs.title: 1.3 Brand-Specific Title Patterns
docs.text:
Hino Parts: [Product] Truk Hino 500/Ranger/Dutro [Series] [Part Number] [Quality] [Warranty]

Examples: - Injector Nozzle Truk Hino 500 J08E G3S12 Liwei Quality - Grosir - Gasket Set Full Truk Hino Lohan FM260 J08E Non-TI NPR - Alternator Dinamo Amper Truk Hino 500 J08E 24V 60A Original Type

Isuzu Parts: [Product] Truk Isuzu [Series/NMR/ELF/Giga] [Engine] [Part Number] [Quality]

Examples: - Injector Assembly Truk Isuzu ELF NKR/NMR 4HK1-TC G3S60 Denso - Piston Ring Set Truk Isuzu NMR 81 4HK1 NPR Quality - Made in Japan - Water Pump Truk Isuzu Giga 6HK1 8-97376-787-0 - Sparepart Diesel

Mitsubishi Parts: [Product] Truk Mitsubishi [Canter/Fighter/Fuso] [Engine] [Part Number]

Examples: - Injector Truk Mitsubishi Canter FE71 4M50 Euro 4 Bosch Type - Piston Ring Set Truk Mitsubishi Fighter 6M60 NPR Japan Quality - Turbocharger Truk Mitsubishi Fighter 6M60 TD06 Original Type

docs.title: 2. TOP 100 SEO KEYWORDS FOR TRUCK PARTS
2.1 Most Searched Keywords
docs.text:

docs.title: 3.1 Tokopedia Attribute Optimization
docs.text:
Required Attributes: - Merek: Aftermarket brand OR "Compatible" - Model: Hino 500, Isuzu ELF, etc. - Tipe: Engine model: J08E, 4HK1, etc. - Kondisi: Baru / Bekas (Copotan) / Rekondisi - Berat: Actual weight in grams - Dimensi: L x W x H in cm - SKU: Your internal code

Recommended Attributes: - Original: Asli / OEM / Aftermarket - Garansi: 7 hari / 14 hari / 30 hari / 6 bulan - Asal Produk: Jepang / Korea / Taiwan / China / Lokal - Kategori: Mesin / Rem / Kopling / Listrik

docs.title: 3.2 Shopee Attribute Optimization
docs.text:
Key Differences from Tokopedia: - More focus on "Ship From" location - "Variation" feature for multiple SKUs - Product condition must match title

SKU Naming Convention: [BRAND]-[CATEGORY]-[PARTNUMBER]-[VARIANT]

Example: - HNO-ENG-J08EINJ-STD (Hino Engine J08E Injector Standard) - ISZ-BRK-4HK1PAD-ORG (Isuzu Brake 4HK1 Pad Original)

docs.title: 3.3 TikTok Shop Attributes
docs.text:
Unique Requirements: - Video thumbnail is CRITICAL - Product must have physical video - "Spark Ads" for promotion - Live streaming capability

TikTok Tags: spareparttruk hino500 isuzunmr jualsparepart grosirsparepart trukindonesia trukdiesel bengkeltruk mechanic truckparts

docs.text:

docs.title: 5.1 Keyword Placement
docs.text: In Title: Primary keyword near beginning In Description: Use keywords naturally, 5-10 times In Attributes: Fill all relevant attribute fields In Images: Filename should include keywords

docs.title: 5.3 Reviews Management
docs.text: Encourage reviews by: - Following up after delivery - Offering small discount on next purchase - Responding to all reviews

docs.text:
Primary Image: - Size: min 500x500px, max 3000x3000px - Background: White (preferred) - Product centered and well-lit

Secondary Images (max 8): - Image 2: Side/back view - Image 3: Detail/close-up - Image 4: Part number/label - Image 5: Packaging - Image 6: Application diagram

docs.text: Cost Price: Rp X Markup: 20-50% (depending on category) Platform Fee: 1-4% (Tokopedia), 2-5% (Shopee) Final Price: Rp Y

docs.title: 7.2 Psychological Pricing
docs.text: Effective Strategies: - End in 000 (Rp 975,000) - Show Was/Now for discounts - Free shipping threshold

Weekly: - Review low-performing listings - Update descriptions based on questions - Check competitor pricing

docs.title: Restricted and Prohibited Products - Commercial Vehicle Parts
Indonesia Automotive Spare Parts Compliance Guide
docs.text: Scope: Tokopedia, Shopee, TikTok Shop, Bukalapak marketplace regulations Focus: Commercial Vehicle Parts (Trucks, Buses, Heavy Equipment) Last Updated: April 8, 2026

docs.title: 1.1 SNI Mandatory Categories for Commercial Vehicles
docs.text: Part Category SNI Required Certification Body Validity Penalty for Non-Compliance Brake Lining (Kampas Rem) YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Product takedown, shop ban Brake Pads YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Product takedown, shop ban Brake Shoes YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Product takedown, shop ban Brake Drum NO - - SNI recommended Steering Components YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Product takedown Suspension Arms YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Product takedown Tire Valves YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Warning, then ban Horn/Air Horn NO - - Must meet noise regulations Headlamp/Taillamp YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Product takedown Windshield Glass NO S-Glass mark - SNI recommended Seat Belts YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Product takedown Reflectors YES BSN/LSPro 4 years Takedown

docs.title: 1.2 SNI Certification Process for Truck Parts
docs.text:
Step 1: Document Preparation - Company NPWP and NIB - Factory audit report (if manufacturer) - Product technical specification - Test samples (minimum 3 units)

Step 2: Laboratory Testing - Submit to accredited lab (LEM, Sucofindo, etc.) - Brake lining friction coefficient test - Heat resistance test (for brake parts) - Impact resistance test (for steering) Testing cost: Rp 5-15 million per product type

Step 3: Certification Audit - Factory inspection (2-3 days) - Quality management system review - Corrective action if needed

Step 4: SNI Mark Grant - Certificate issued by BSN - Valid for 4 years (annual surveillance) - SNI logo can be printed on packaging

Costs: | Part Type | Testing Cost | Audit Cost | Total | |-----------|--------------|------------|-------| | Brake Lining | Rp 8-12M | Rp 10-15M | Rp 18-27M | | Steering Parts | Rp 5-8M | Rp 10-15M | Rp 15-23M | | Lighting | Rp 5-7M | Rp 8-12M | Rp 13-19M |

docs.title: 2.1 Batteries (Aki/Aki Truck)
docs.text:
Classification: B3 (Bahan Berbahaya dan Beracun)

Battery Type Air Shipping Land Shipping Labeling Dry (MF) Battery Restricted Allowed with B3 label UN 2794 Wet/Flooded Battery PROHIBITED Allowed with B3 + Corrosive label UN 2796 Li-ion Battery Restricted (DGR compliant) Allowed UN 3480 AGM/Gel Battery Restricted Allowed UN 2800

Required Documentation: - Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) - B3 shipping declaration - UN38.3 test report (for lithium batteries) - Dangerous Goods Declaration

Courier Restrictions: - JNE YES: NO batteries over 50Wh - JNE REG: Allowed with B3 label - Cargo/Cargo Truck: Recommended for bulk batteries - SiCepat BEST: NO batteries

Packaging Requirements: - Acid-resistant container - UN-certified corrugated box - Absorbent material for leaks - "B3" and "Dilarang Dibuang Sembarangan" labels

docs.title: 2.2 Brake Fluid (Minyak Rem)
docs.text:
Classification: B3 - Flammable Liquid (Class 3)

Product Tokopedia Listing Shipping Method Brake Fluid DOT 3 Allowed with warning Land only (JTR/Cargo) Brake Fluid DOT 4 Allowed with warning Land only Brake Fluid DOT 5.1 Allowed with warning Land only

Storage Requirements: - Keep away from ignition sources - Store below 40C - Proper ventilation - Spill containment kit

docs.title: 2.3 Engine Oil and Lubricants
docs.text:
Classification: Non-hazardous (above flash point)

Product Shipping Notes Engine Oil All couriers Original packaging only Hydraulic Oil All couriers Under 20L per package Gear Oil (Mtf) All couriers Original packaging Coolant/Anti-freeze Land couriers B3 if ethylene glycol >10%

Prohibited: Repackaged oil (must be original sealed container)

docs.title: 2.4 Tire Repair Materials
docs.text: Product Shipping Status Reason Tire Repair Patch Restricted Some contain solvents Tire Sealant (Aerosol) Restricted Pressurized container Rubber Cement Restricted Flammable solvent Valve Cores Allowed -

docs.title: 3.1 High Risk (Immediate Ban Risk)
docs.text: Part Risk Level Common Violation Prevention Counterfeit Brake Parts CRITICAL Fake SNI mark Buy only from authorized distributors Used Airbags CRITICAL Safety recall parts Do not sell airbags Modified Turbo HIGH Illegal emissions delete Verify EPA compliance Non-SNI Brake Lining HIGH No certification Get SNI before listing Repacked Oil HIGH Originality violation Only sell sealed containers

docs.title: 3.2 Medium Risk (Warning/Suspension)
docs.text: Part Risk Level Issue Solution Aftermarket Electrical MEDIUM Compatibility claims State "For replacement only" Used Sensors MEDIUM Functionality uncertain Test and warranty Import Tools MEDIUM No Indonesian labeling Add Indonesian manual Performance Parts MEDIUM Road legal questions Check DOT/E-mark

docs.title: 3.3 Low Risk (Documentation Only)
docs.text: Part Notes Filters Generally safe Gaskets No certification required Bearings Standard parts Fasteners Baut, mur standard Hoses Standard replacements

docs.title: 4.1 Tokopedia Restricted Items
docs.text:
Strictly PROHIBITED: - Airbag components - SRS modules - Seatbelt pretensioners - Crash sensors - Any pyrotechnic safety device

RESTRICTED (with conditions): - Brake fluids (original sealed only) - Batteries (B3 labeling required) - Tire inflators (pressure limit 150 PSI) - Engine oil (original packaging)

MONITORING KEYWORDS: Tokopedia bots scan for: - "Airbag" - "KW" (fake/counterfeit) - "Copotan" (used parts - allowed but flagged) - Brand names without authorization

docs.title: 4.2 Shopee Restricted Items
docs.text:
PROHIBITED: - Hazardous materials without proper labeling - Stolen vehicle parts (VIN check) - Fake SNI products - Recalled parts

REQUIRES PERMIT: - Bulk lubricants (>100L) - Commercial quantity batteries (>20 units) - Wholesale tire sales (import documents)

docs.title: 4.3 TikTok Shop Restrictions
docs.text:
Live Selling Banned: - Batteries (fire hazard demonstration) - Pressurized cans - Liquids (spillage risk)

Allowed with Caution: - Filters (no liquid) - Dry parts (brake pads, clutch) - Electrical (if properly packaged)

docs.title: 5.1 Required Documents by Part Value
docs.text: Part Value Required Documents Under Rp 500K Basic invoice (supplier) Rp 500K - 2M Supplier invoice + COA Rp 2M - 10M Supplier invoice + COA + Import docs (if applicable) Above Rp 10M Full paper trail + SNI (if required) + Warranty

docs.title: 5.2 Import Documentation (PIB)
docs.text:
For Imported Parts: - PIB (Pemberitahuan Impor Barang) - Packing list - Commercial invoice - Bill of lading - Import permit (if restricted)

Digitization: - Scan at 300 DPI minimum - Store in cloud (Google Drive/Dropbox) - Keep for 7 years (tax requirement)

docs.title: 5.3 SNI Certificate Display
docs.text: For SNI-certified products: - Upload SNI certificate as product image - Include SNI logo in description - Display certificate number - Valid until date must be visible

docs.title: 6.1 Immediate Actions
docs.text:
1. DO NOT RELIST immediately (triggers spam detection)
  2. Review Tokopedia email for specific reason
  3. Check product against restricted list
  4. Gather supporting documents

docs.title: 6.2 Appeal Process
docs.text:
Step 1: Document Review - Check if SNI is current - Verify brand authorization - Confirm hazmat compliance

Step 2: Submit Appeal - Go to Tokopedia Seller Center > Pengaturan > Laporkan - Select category: "Produk dihapus" - Upload supporting docs - Explain corrective action

Step 3: Follow Up - Response time: 1-3 business days - Escalate if no response in 5 days - Live chat follow-up recommended

docs.title: 6.3 Prevention Checklist
docs.text: Before listing any part, verify: - [ ] Is SNI required? If yes, do I have valid certificate? - [ ] Is this a hazmat item? If yes, proper labeling? - [ ] Are images original (not copied)? - [ ] Is description accurate (no exaggerated claims)? - [ ] Do I have supplier invoice as proof? - [ ] Is this a recalled part? (check sp.go.id) - [ ] Am I using any copyrighted brand images?

docs.title: 7.1 Single Product Certification
docs.text: Cost Item Brake Lining Lighting Steering Lab Testing Rp 8-12M Rp 5-7M Rp 5-8M Factory Audit Rp 10-15M Rp 8-12M Rp 10-15M Certificate Fee Rp 2-3M Rp 2-3M Rp 2-3M Annual Surveillance Rp 3-5M Rp 3-5M Rp 3-5M TOTAL Rp 23-35M Rp 18-27M Rp 20-31M

docs.title: 7.2 OEM vs Aftermarket Certification
docs.text: Brand Type Certification Path Timeline OEM (Hino, Isuzu, etc.) Principal certifies Done by brand Aftermarket Taiwan Import and test 3-4 months Local Brand Full certification 3-6 months Generic/Unbranded Cannot certify Consider private label

docs.title: 7.3 Cost Recovery Strategy
docs.text: Approach Investment ROI Timeline Certify top 5 SKUs Rp 100-150M 12-18 months Focus on brake parts only Rp 50-80M 8-12 months Partner with certified supplier Rp 0 Immediate

docs.text:
• Review marketplace policy updates
  • Check SNI certificate expiration dates
  • Audit product listings for accuracy

docs.text:

  • Review hazmat shipping compliance
  • Check for recall notifications

  • Insurance review
  • Full compliance audit

docs.title: Shipping and Fulfillment Guide - Commercial Vehicle Spare Parts
Heavy Parts Logistics, Packaging, and Cost Optimization
docs.text: Last Updated: April 8, 2026 Scope: Tokopedia, Shopee, TikTok Shop shipping methods

docs.title: 1.1 Domestic Courier Comparison
docs.text:
Courier Max Weight Max Dimension Price per kg Best For JNE YES 50kg 150x100x100cm Rp 15K-25K Medium parts, fast delivery JNE REG 70kg 200x150x150cm Rp 8K-12K Heavy parts, standard JNE JTR 150kg Unlimited Rp 6K-8K Very heavy parts JNT 50kg 100x80x80cm Rp 12K-18K Medium parts SiCepat BEST 20kg 80x60x60cm Rp 10K-15K Small-medium parts Ninja Xpress 50kg 100x80x80cm Rp 11K-16K Medium parts

For Parts over 50kg: | Service | Max Weight | Cost Range | |---------|----------|------------| | JNE Cargo | 500kg+ | Rp 5K-8K/kg | | Dakota Cargo | 1000kg+ | Rp 4K-7K/kg | | Deliveree | Truck | Rp 300K-2M |

docs.title: 1.2 Inter-Island Shipping
docs.text: Destination Courier Cost per kg Est. Days Surabaya JNE Rp 8K-12K 2-4 Medan JNE Rp 12K-18K 3-5 Makassar JNE Rp 18K-25K 4-7 Balikpapan JNE Rp 20K-28K 5-8 Kupang JNE Rp 28K-40K 7-10

docs.title: 2.1 Fragile Parts
docs.text:
Required Materials: - Bubble wrap (minimum 3 layers) - Corrugated box (double wall for over 10kg) - Foam inserts - Fragile stickers

Packaging Steps: 1. Wrap item individually in bubble wrap 2. Place in box with foam padding (minimum 5cm) 3. Fill voids with packing peanuts 4. Double-box for items over Rp 1M value 5. Apply fragile stickers

Examples: | Part Type | Inner Wrapping | Outer Box | Protection | |-----------|----------------|-----------|------------| | Injector | Bubble wrap + ziplock | Corrugated + foam | Double box | | ECU | Anti-static bag + foam | Thick corrugated | Fragile sticker | | Alternator | Bubble wrap corners | Standard box | Reinforce heavy side | | Sensors | Individual boxes | Master carton | Foam layer | | Glass/Headlamp | Foam sleeve + wrap | Double wall box | Fragile sticker |

docs.title: 2.2 Bulk/Heavy Parts
docs.text:
Required Materials: - Cardboard sheets - Stretch/pallet wrap - Edge protectors - Wooden pallet (for over 50kg) - Strapping tape

Packaging Steps: 1. Clean part thoroughly 2. Apply rust inhibitor if steel 3. Wrap in corrugated sheets 4. Secure with stretch wrap 5. Add edge protectors 6. Strap to pallet for heavy items

Examples: | Part Type | Weight | Packaging Method | Cost | |-----------|--------|------------------|------| | Cylinder Head | 15-25kg | Box with foam | Rp 50K-80K | | Engine Block | 50-150kg | Pallet + crate | Rp 200K-500K | | Transmission | 80-200kg | Pallet + strapping | Rp 250K-600K | | Brake Drum | 10-20kg | Box + foam | Rp 40K-70K | | Turbocharger | 5-10kg | Box with foam | Rp 30K-50K |

docs.title: 2.3 Hazmat Parts
docs.text:
Battery Packaging (UN 2794/2796): - Acid-resistant tray - Absorbent material - B3 label - Corrosion warning - UN-compliant outer box

Brake Fluid (Flammable): - Original sealed containers - Individual plastic bags - Separation from other items - Flammable label

Oil/Lubricant: - Original containers - Cap seal intact - Plastic bag wrap - Absorbent paper

docs.title: 3.1 Volumetric Weight Formula
docs.text:
Standard Formula: Volumetric Weight (kg) = (Length x Width x Height in cm) / Divider

Dividers by courier: - JNE: 6,000 - JNT: 5,000 - SiCepat: 5,000

Example: Part: Gasket set box (40cm x 30cm x 10cm), Actual weight: 500g

JNE calculation: (40 x 30 x 10) / 6,000 = 12,000 / 6,000 = 2kg (volumetric)

Billed: 2kg (volumetric greater than actual)

docs.title: 3.2 Dimensional Weight Examples
docs.text: Part Actual Dim Actual Wt Vol Wt (JNE) Billed Wt Filter set 25x20x15cm 300g 1.25kg 1.25kg Gasket set 40x30x10cm 500g 2kg 2kg Brake pads 30x20x8cm 1kg 0.8kg 1kg Radiator 80x60x15cm 5kg 12kg 12kg

docs.title: 3.3 Optimization Strategies
docs.text:
Reduce Volumetric Weight: 1. Compact packaging (remove air gaps) 2. Flat packaging for flat parts 3. Vacuum bags where possible 4. Remove unnecessary packaging 5. Use custom box sizes

Cost Savings Example: Original: 40x30x15 box = 3kg volumetric Optimized: 35x25x12 box = 1.75kg volumetric

Savings per shipment: 1.25kg x Rp 12,000 = Rp 15,000

docs.title: 4.1 Required Label Information
docs.text: Standard Shipping Label: - From: Nama Toko, Alamat, No. Telp - To: Nama Penerima, Alamat, No. Telp - Weight: X kg - Dimensions: LxWxH cm - Invoice: INV-XXX - Barcode

docs.title: 4.2 Handling Labels
docs.text: Must Use for: | Label | When to Use | |-------|-------------| | FRAGILE | All electrical, sensors, glass | | THIS SIDE UP | Liquids, fragile electronics | | HEAVY | Items over 20kg | | HANDLE WITH CARE | All mechanical parts |

docs.title: 5.1 Order Processing Timeline
docs.text:
Standard SLAs: | Platform | Pickup SLA | Ship By | |----------|------------|---------| | Tokopedia | Next day | Day 1-2 | | Shopee | Same day | Day 0-1 | | TikTok | Next day | Day 1-2 |

Internal Workflow: Hour 0-1: Order received, verify stock Hour 1-2: Pick items from warehouse Hour 2-3: Quality check + packaging Hour 3-4: Label and stage for pickup Hour 4-24: Courier pickup

docs.title: 5.2 Warehouse Layout
docs.text:
Zone Layout: - Zone A: Receiving - Zone B: Storage - Zone C: Picking (fast-moving) - Zone D: Packing - Zone E: Shipping

Fast-Moving Zone: - Best sellers within arm reach - Eye-level shelving - Quick access - Same-day ship items

docs.title: 5.3 Pick List Generation
docs.text: Sample Pick List: - Order Number: TKP-12345 - Date: [Date] - Items: SKU, Description, Qty, Check

docs.title: 6.1 Damaged Goods Handling
docs.text:
Prevention: - Proper packaging per category - Insurance for high-value items - Photo documentation before ship - Fragile labeling

Resolution Steps: 1. Request unboxing video from buyer 2. Review packaging adequacy 3. File insurance claim (if insured) 4. Offer partial refund or replacement 5. Update packaging standards

docs.title: 6.2 Lost Package Handling
docs.text:
Tracking Protocol: 1. Check courier tracking first 2. Contact courier CS (48h after expected) 3. File claim with platform (72h delay) 4. Escalate if over 7 days 5. Offer replacement or refund

Insurance Coverage: | Platform | Auto Insurance | Max Claim | |----------|---------------|-----------| | Tokopedia | Up to Rp 1M | Rp 10M | | Shopee | Up to Rp 2M | Rp 10M |

docs.title: 6.3 Delivery Failed/RTS
docs.text: Reshipment Process: 1. Confirm correct address with buyer 2. Check if buyer wants to proceed 3. Generate new label (charge buyer for second) 4. Update order status 5. Monitor closely

docs.title: 7.1 Packaging Cost Reduction
docs.text: Bulk Packaging Buying: - Boxes: Buy 100+ for 30 percent discount - Bubble wrap: 100m roll vs per-meter - Tape: Bulk cases - Labels: Print in-house after 100+ daily

docs.title: 7.2 Shipping Discount Programs
docs.text:
Tokopedia: - Power Merchant: 5-15 percent discount - Free shipping subsidy program - Bulk label printing discounts

Shopee: - Free shipping XTRA programs - Coins cashback - ShopeePay discounts

Direct Courier Contracts: - JNE Corporate: Volume discounts - Monthly minimum commitments

docs.title: 7.3 Consolidation Shipping
docs.text: Multi-Item Orders: - Combine into one box when possible - Use single tracking number - Clear Combined Package note - Slight discount for buyer incentive

docs.title: 8.1 Import Documentation
docs.text: Required for Parts from Abroad: - PIB (Pemberitahuan Impor Barang) - Packing list - Commercial invoice - Bill of lading/airway bill - HS Code classification

docs.title: 8.2 Export Documentation
docs.text: For Sales to Malaysia/Singapore: - Export permit - Certificate of origin - Customs declaration - Commercial invoice

docs.title: 9.1 Same-Day Delivery
docs.text:
Services: - Gojek instant (Jabodetabek) - GrabExpress instant - Tokopedia NOW

Requirements: - Order before 12:00 - Stock ready - Packaging pre-staged - Premium: Rp 15K-50K

docs.title: 9.2 COD Considerations
docs.text: COD Handling: - Verify buyer availability - Clear delivery window - Mobile payment devices - Float money for change

docs.title: 10.1 Key Performance Indicators
docs.text: Metric Target Industry Avg Order processing time under 4 hours 12-24 hours Same-day ship rate over 50 percent 20-30 percent Damage rate under 0.5 percent 1-2 percent Return rate under 2 percent 5-10 percent

docs.title: 10.2 Measurement Tools
docs.text:
Tracking: - Tokopedia/seller dashboard - Shopee seller center - Spreadsheet daily log - Weekly trend analysis

Part Numbers CRITICAL for Commercial Exact OEM numbers in titles = mechanics trust you 4. Pricing Strategy MEDIUM Budget (Bohlamhid) to Premium (Shell) - both succeed 5. Multi-Platform MEDIUM Tokopedia + Shopee + TikTok = maximum reach 6. B2B Focus HIGH for Commercial Reseller programs, wholesale pricing, WhatsApp contact 7. docs.title: PART D: KEY LESSONS FOR YOUR SHOP
For Consumer Car Parts
docs.text:
1. One-Stop Shop Strategy (Jasutra Model)
  2. Carry tires, filters, oil, bulbs, wipers, car care
  3. Customers buy multiple items per transaction

  4. Higher average order value (AOV)

  5. Niche Dominance Strategy (Bohlamhid Model)

  6. Own ONE category (spark plugs, filters, etc.)
  7. Build brand authority

  8. Offer reseller program for B2B growth

  9. OEM Authority Strategy (DENSO Model)

  10. Partner with OEM brands (DENSO, NGK, T-OPT)
  11. Highlight manufacturer relationships

  12. Target workshops needing genuine parts for warranty repairs

  13. Multi-Platform Presence

  14. All top sellers are on Tokopedia + Shopee + TikTok
  15. Different platforms = different customer segments

  16. TikTok Shop = younger buyers, Tokopedia = older/professional

  17. Vehicle Compatibility in Titles

  18. Include car models in product titles
  19. Example: "Busi NGK Innova Fortuner" not just "Busi NGK"
  20. Improves search visibility

docs.title: For Commercial Vehicle Parts
docs.text:
1. Technical Accuracy is Non-Negotiable
  2. Display exact OEM part numbers
  3. Include engine codes (4HK1, J08E, J08C)

  4. Fleet managers search by part number, not description

  5. EURO 4/5 Specialization = Higher Margin

  6. Modern trucks require specialized parts
  7. Lower competition due to complexity

  8. Higher margins (Rp1.9M - Rp6.25M per item)

  9. Offer OEM + Aftermarket Options

  10. Budget customers: Sakura, Hengst, aftermarket
  11. Premium customers: WABCO, BOSCH, genuine parts

  12. Capture both market segments

  13. Warranty = Trust

  14. Commercial buyers need assurance for expensive parts
  15. Offer warranty for OEM parts

  16. "Garansi Original" in product titles

  17. Multi-Brand Coverage

  18. Don't limit to one truck brand
  19. Cover HINO + Isuzu + Mitsubishi + Mercedes
  20. Fleet operators have mixed fleets

docs.title: Pricing Recommendations
docs.text: Category Recommended Strategy Consumer - Recurring Items (filters, spark plugs) Budget to mid-range - compete on price Consumer - Premium Items (tires, batteries) Mid-range - balance price vs. trust Consumer - Accessories (car mats, bulbs) Budget - impulse purchases Commercial - Filters Both OEM + aftermarket options Commercial - EURO 4 Parts Premium - specialized, low competition Commercial - Major Components (turbo, injectors) Premium with warranty

docs.title: Car Spare Parts Market - Indonesia | April 2026
docs.text: Research Date: April 7, 2026 Data Sources: Tokopedia, Bank Indonesia, Gaikindo, ICCT, Custom Market Insights

docs.title: 1. TOP 10 BEST-SELLING SPARE PARTS ON TOKOPEDIA
docs.text:
Based on market research and sales volume data from Tokopedia:

Rank Product Category Estimated Monthly Volume Competition Margin Potential 1 Oli Mesin / Engine Oil Highest (by volume) VERY HIGH Low 2 Filter Oli / Oil Filter 4,000+ per top seller HIGH Medium 3 Kampas Rem / Brake Pads 4,000+ per top seller HIGH Medium 4 Filter Udara / Air Filter 2,000+ per top seller HIGH Medium 5 Aki / Battery High (GS Astra, Aspira) MEDIUM High 6 Busi / Spark Plugs 1,000+ per top seller HIGH Medium 7 Wiper Blade 1,000+ per top seller MEDIUM Medium 8 Filter AC / Cabin Filter 2,000+ per top seller MEDIUM Medium 9 Minyak Rem / Brake Fluid 4,000+ per top seller HIGH Low 10 Lampu / Bulbs (HID/LED) High (bohlamhid) HIGH Medium

docs.title: Key Insights:
docs.text:
• Filter Oli and Kampas Rem consistently hit 4,000+ units sold per top seller
  • Aki GS / Aspira (Astra Otoparts brands) dominate battery category
  • Oli Mesin has highest volume but lowest margin — enter only for traffic
  • T-OPT (Toyota's alternative brand) grew 3x from 2024-2025, now in 700+ stores

docs.title: A. Mudik / Lebaran Season (Peak: March-April)
docs.text:
Metric Data Sales Increase 20%+ for direct-to-consumer sales Golden Moment Before mudik + After mudik (return trip) Duration Extends several weeks post-Lebaran Source PT Astra Otoparts (GridOto interview)

Top Products During Mudik: - Brake pads & brake shoes (safety check before long trip) - Oil filter + engine oil (pre-trip service) - Wiper blades (rainy season overlap) - Suspension parts (heavy load preparation) - Tires (safety replacement)

Strategy: Stock up 1 month before Ramadan. Run promos 2 weeks before estimated mudik dates.

docs.title: B. Year-End Holiday (Peak: November-December)
docs.text:
Metric Data RSI Growth 4.3% yoy (Oct 2025: 219.7 index) Key Drivers Christmas, New Year, Year-End Bonuses Spare Parts Sales +7.5% yoy (July 2025 data, Bank Indonesia)

Top Products During Year-End: - Vehicle accessories (gift purchases) - Premium replacement parts (bonus spending) - Car care products (polish, detailing) - Battery replacements (rainy season overlap)

Strategy: Premium products sell better — consumers have more budget. Bundle accessories with maintenance items.

docs.title: C. Rainy Season (Peak: November-February)
docs.text:
Metric Data Key Categories Wipers, AC/Cabin filters, Brakes Manufacturer Response Toyota, Mitsubishi, Suzuki run rainy season promos

Top Products During Rainy Season: | Product | Reason | Promo Activity | |---------|--------|----------------| | Wiper Blades | Visibility safety | Toyota: 10% discount | | Cabin/AC Filter | Humidity, mold prevention | Bosch: active marketing | | Brake Pads | Wet road braking | Mitsubishi: Rainy Campaign | | Tires | Wet traction safety | Various tire promos | | Car covers | Parking protection | Seasonal demand |

Strategy: Stock wipers and cabin filters from October. Partner with brands running official rainy season campaigns.

docs.title: D. Beginning of School Year (Peak: July-August)
docs.text:
Metric Data Consumer Segment Parents, students (motorcycle focus) Key Drivers New semester, student transportation needs Spare Parts Focus Two-wheeler maintenance

Top Products: - Motorcycle spare parts (brake pads, chains, tires) - Helmet accessories - Basic car maintenance (family vehicles for school runs)

Note: This season has LESS impact on commercial vehicle parts. Focus on consumer/motorcycle segment.

docs.title: EURO 4 Transition Impact
docs.text:
Status Details Implementation Gradual since 2021, national mandate ongoing Mining Sector Enforcement tightened for foreign trucks Market Effect New demand for EURO 4 specific parts

EURO 4 Specific Parts (Growing Demand):

Product Price Range Growth Driver Common Rail Injector Rp 2.3M - Rp 7M EURO 4 engine requirement Fuel Filter (Common Rail) Rp 135K - Rp 4.35M High-pressure fuel system Diesel Particulate Filter Premium Emission compliance EGR Valve Components Medium-High Emission control

Commercial Vehicle Market Context: - Heavy-duty vehicle parts aftermarket: USD 92.73B in 2025 (global) - Indonesian aftermarket: USD 4.82B in 2025, growing to USD 7.59B by 2034 - CAGR: 4.78% - Vehicle parks expanding 5-6% annually until 2030

EURO 4 Opportunity Assessment:

Factor Impact Fleet Renewal Older trucks being replaced with EURO 4 models Parts Complexity More expensive, specialized components Competition LOWER — specialized knowledge required Margin HIGHER — technical products, fewer sellers Customer Loyalty HIGH — fleet operators return to trusted suppliers

docs.title: A. Recurring Purchase Products (Predictable Revenue)
docs.text:
Product Replacement Interval Annual Revenue Potential Oil Filter 10,000-15,000 km or 6 months 2x per vehicle per year Engine Oil 5,000-10,000 km 2-3x per vehicle per year Air Filter 15,000-20,000 km 1x per vehicle per year Cabin/AC Filter 15,000-20,000 km 1x per vehicle per year Spark Plugs (Standard) ~20,000 km 1x per vehicle per year Spark Plugs (Iridium) Up to 50,000 km 1x per 2-3 years Brake Pads 40,000-80,000 km 1x per 1-2 years Brake Fluid 40,000 km or 2 years 1x per 2 years Wiper Blades 6-12 months 1-2x per year

Recurring Revenue Strategy: - Build customer database with service reminders - Offer subscription bundles (oil + filter package) - Partner with workshops for regular referrals

docs.text:
Product Replacement Interval Margin Competition Timing Belt 80,000-120,000 km HIGH MEDIUM Clutch Plate 80,000-100,000 km HIGH MEDIUM Battery 2-3 years MEDIUM HIGH Tires 3-5 years MEDIUM HIGH Alternator/Starter 5-10 years HIGH LOW Radiator 10-15 years HIGH LOW Injector (EURO 4) Failure-based VERY HIGH LOW Suspension Parts 5+ years HIGH MEDIUM

docs.title: Competitive Positioning
docs.text: Segment Strategy Consumer Car Parts Compete on price + bundling; focus on recurring customers Commercial Vehicle Parts Compete on expertise; stock EURO 4 specific parts Premium/Accessories Compete on quality; target year-end season Emerging (EV/Hybrid) Build knowledge; prepare for 2028+ market

docs.title: Indonesian Commercial Vehicle Emission Standards & Spare Parts Market | April 2026
docs.text: Research Date: April 7, 2026 Focus: Emission standard transition, parts differences, market opportunities for spare parts sellers

docs.title: Key Dates
docs.text: Milestone Date Details Regulation signed March 10, 2017 Permen LHK No. P.20/MENLHK/SETJEN/KUM.1/3/2017 Gasoline vehicles September 2018 Phase 1 implementation Diesel vehicles (original target) September 2020 Phase 2 - delayed Diesel vehicles (actual) April 7, 2022 Official implementation for all diesel vehicles Future target: Euro 5 2025+ Planned adoption

docs.title: Why the Delay?
docs.text: Factor Impact Pertamina fuel readiness Required sulfur ≤50ppm diesel production Manufacturer preparation Time needed to develop Euro 4 models Distribution infrastructure Ensuring fuel availability nationwide Testing equipment Kemenhub needed time to prepare type-testing facilities

docs.title: Legal Basis
docs.text: Regulation Purpose Permen LHK P.20/2017 Original emission standard mandate Surat KLHK No S.786/2020 Confirmed April 2022 implementation RUEN (Rencana Umum Energi Nasional) National energy plan backing Euro 4

docs.title: Emission Limits Comparison
docs.text: Parameter Euro 2 (2005) Euro 4 (2022) Reduction CO (Carbon Monoxide) 4.0 g/kWh 1.5 g/kWh 62.5% HC (Hydrocarbons) 1.1 g/kWh 0.46 g/kWh 58% NOx (Nitrogen Oxides) 7.0 g/kWh 3.5 g/kWh 50% PM (Particulate Matter) 0.15 g/kWh 0.02 g/kWh 87%

docs.title: Fuel Requirements
docs.text: Specification Euro 2 Euro 4 Sulfur content ≤500 ppm ≤50 ppm Cetane number No minimum Minimum 51 Benzene content ≤5% ≤1%

docs.title: Key Technology Differences
docs.text: System Euro 2 Euro 4 Fuel Injection Mechanical / EUI (Electronic Unit Injector) Common Rail (high-pressure) Injection Pressure 200-400 bar 1,600-2,500 bar ECU Integration Basic ECU Integrated ECU + EDU Emissions Control Minimal EGR / SCR / DPF OBD (On-Board Diagnostics) Basic OBD-II with full monitoring

docs.title: 3. PARTS DIFFERENT BETWEEN EURO 2 AND EURO 4
Major Component Changes
docs.text: Component Euro 2 Euro 4 Change Impact Injector EUI / Mechanical Common Rail (Denso/Bosch) Higher precision, higher cost Fuel Pump Mechanical lift pump High-pressure common rail pump Complex, expensive ECU (Engine Control Unit) Basic control Integrated ECU + EDU More sensors, more codes Turbocharger Fixed geometry Variable Geometry Turbo (VGT) Better response, more complex EGR System None or basic EGR valve + cooler Reduces NOx, requires maintenance DPF (Diesel Particulate Filter) None Standard on most models Filters soot, requires regeneration SCR System None Selective Catalytic Reduction Uses AdBlue/DEF NOx Sensor None 1-2 sensors per vehicle Monitors emissions Oxygen Sensor Basic Wideband O2 sensor Precise fuel control Rail Pressure Sensor None High-precision sensor Critical for common rail

docs.title: Euro 2: Mechanical / EUI System
docs.text:
[Fuel Tank] → [Lift Pump] → [Mechanical Pump] → [EUI Injectors]
                                          ↓
                                    [Return Line]

docs.title: Euro 4: Common Rail System
docs.text:
[Fuel Tank] → [Electric Lift Pump] → [High-Pressure Pump] → [Common Rail] → [Injectors]
         ↑                                                            ↓
         └────────────────── [Return Line] ←─────────────────────────┘

Additional Components:
- Rail Pressure Sensor
- Pressure Control Valve
- Fuel Temperature Sensor
- Multiple Injection Events ( Pilot, Main, Post )

docs.title: Engine Management Differences
docs.text: Aspect Euro 2 Euro 4 Injection timing Fixed or basic electronic Fully electronic, variable Injection events Single injection Multiple (pilot, main, post) Fuel quantity control Mechanical governor ECU-controlled with feedback Diagnostics Limited codes Full OBD-II with live data Sensor count 5-10 sensors 15-25 sensors

docs.title: 4. EURO 4-SPECIFIC PARTS IN HIGH DEMAND & LOW SUPPLY
High-Demand, Low-Supply Parts
docs.text: Part Demand Level Supply Level Price Range (OEM) Notes Common Rail Injector HIGH LOW Rp 8-25M each Most critical, fails often High-Pressure Pump HIGH LOW Rp 15-40M Complex, needs precision DPF Filter MEDIUM LOW Rp 25-60M Clogging common, expensive NOx Sensor MEDIUM LOW Rp 3-8M each Often needs 2 per truck EGR Valve + Cooler MEDIUM MEDIUM Rp 5-15M Carbon buildup issues Rail Pressure Sensor HIGH MEDIUM Rp 2-5M Fails frequently VGT Actuator MEDIUM LOW Rp 8-20M Electronic turbo control ECU/EDU Module MEDIUM LOW Rp 20-80M Programming required AdBlue Injector MEDIUM LOW Rp 3-8M SCR system component AdBlue Pump MEDIUM LOW Rp 10-25M SCR system component

docs.text: Part Origin Lead Time ECU/EDU Modules Japan, Germany 2-4 weeks Common Rail Pumps Japan, Germany 1-3 weeks DPF Filters Japan, China 1-2 weeks VGT Turbochargers Japan, Korea 1-3 weeks SCR Catalyst Japan, Germany 2-4 weeks

docs.title: 5. EURO 2 PARTS: CAN THEY STILL BE SOLD?
Legal Status
docs.text: Aspect Status Sale of Euro 2 parts LEGAL — No restrictions on selling Import of Euro 2 parts LEGAL — Can import for replacement market New vehicle registration BLOCKED — Only Euro 4+ since April 2022 Used vehicle import Age restrictions apply

docs.title: Market Reality
docs.text: Scenario Implication Euro 2 trucks on road Estimated 3-4 million units still operating Parts demand HIGH — Existing fleet needs maintenance Price trend Stable to increasing (scarcity premium) Long-term outlook Gradual decline as fleet ages

docs.title: What This Means for Sellers
docs.text: Action Recommendation Continue selling Euro 2 parts YES — Strong market for 10+ years Stock both Euro 2 and Euro 4 YES — Diversify inventory Focus only on Euro 4 NO — Missing large existing market Phase out Euro 2 parts NOT YET — Demand still strong

docs.title: 6. COMMON EURO 4 ENGINE MODELS IN INDONESIA
Hino (PT Hino Motors Manufacturing Indonesia)
docs.text: Engine Model Application Power Key Features J08E-WE Ranger FM, FG 260 PS @ 2,500 rpm Common rail, EGR J08E-WF Ranger FG, FL 240 PS @ 2,500 rpm Common rail, EGR N04C-T Dutro, Dyna 150 PS Common rail, DPF A09C-UP Ranger SG 350 PS Common rail, SCR

docs.title: Hino ECU/EDU Integration (Euro 4 Innovation)
docs.text:
Hino's 4th generation ECU integrates ECU + EDU into a single unit for better synchronization between engine control and fuel injection.

Key Euro 4 System Components: - Common rail injection system - EGR (Exhaust Gas Recirculation) - DPF (Diesel Particulate Filter) - Integrated ECU-EDU module

docs.title: Isuzu
docs.text: Engine Model Application Power Key Features 4HK1-TCN N-Series (NQR, NPS) 190 PS Common rail, EGR 4HK1-TC N-Series, ELF 155-190 PS Common rail (since 2011) 6HK1-TC Giga, Forward 240-300 PS Common rail, SCR 6WG1-TC Giga Heavy 350-400 PS Common rail, SCR

docs.title: Isuzu Advantage
docs.text: Isuzu had common rail technology since 2011 (Giga series) — 10+ years before Euro 4 mandate. This means: - Mature technology platform - Established parts supply chain - Better aftermarket availability vs competitors
